Uplink update

May 1994
Journalism & NewsroomInvestigative MethodsTechnology

The National Institute for Computer-Assisted Reporting announces expansion efforts including upgrades to its data library, planned seminars, and a computer-assisted day at the upcoming IRE conference in St. Louis. The newsletter invites journalists to submit CAR-inspired stories and offers resources on data storage and banking investigations.

We're forging ahead at the National Institute for Computer-Assisted Reporting as equipment, data and requests for seminars pour in.

In this expanded issue we will report on new developments at the institute that include the upgrading our data library, plans for the optional computer-assisted day at the national IRE conference in St. Louis in June and our increasing number of on-the-road seminars.

This issue also has an overview of how to store all that data that some of you are accumulating, a tech tip from our academic adviser Richard Mullins and two looks at probing banking practices.

In the past few weeks, we've received a number of excellent CAR-inspired stories. Please send us your articles or videos as soon as they appear so we can put them in our resource center and use them at our seminars and conferences.

And if you have any CAR-related questions or suggestions, call us at (314)882-0684 or e-mail us at jourjbh@muccmail.missouri.edu.
